# Extended Case References — Lecture I9uRs8XywRk

*Brief mentions of canonical cases that did not warrant a full `case_index.md` entry. Generated by reconciliation pass against the 1,676-cluster canon. Substantive treatments are in `case_index.md`.*

## Cases mentioned in passing

### Trailer design joint failure example
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "Trailer design joint failure example"
- **Anchor:** `§9.p2`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "We didn't talk about this yet, but compared to my trailer example, here you do have welds. You can't make this complex shape — these are hollow tubes with ball-joint forgings and everything together — in a practical sense without welding them or using a lot of rivets or bolts. The rivets or bolts are too expensive for this application. So it ends up being a lot of welding. And when we're talking about welding, the fatigue becomes more important. That ties back into our first example."

### Tractor-trailer frame weld failures
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "Tractor-trailer frame weld failures"
- **Anchor:** `§13.p3`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "I've talked about some of the other stuff later, but — hey, I sit here and listen to it, I can think of other things I'll talk about. If you talk about bolting on trailers, the big heavy trailers going down the highway — tractor trailers — you are not allowed by code to weld on that frame, that steel frame. Too many trailers have had to be welded — given only welds. Okay, but we'll talk about that. Bolts are heavier, they're not as strong as welds. It takes longer to do — it has to do —"
